title: Ainsi Parlait Zarathoustra
summaries: "Ainsi Parlait Zarathoustra" by Friedrich Nietzsche is a work of philosophical fiction published between 1883 and 1885. Through the character of Zarathustra, based on the ancient Persian prophet Zoroaster, Nietzsche delivers discourses on profound subjects ranging from metaphysics to the nature of existence. The work introduces core concepts including the Übermensch, the death of God, and eternal recurrence. Written in analogical and figurative language, this enigmatic masterpiece invites varied interpretations while challenging conventional morality and received wisdom.
